response.justification: Maria has exceptional technical depth and demonstrated passion for developer productivity and testing frameworks, which aligns well with the role's technical focus. However, the position explicitly requires 2 years of people management experience (conducting 1:1s, setting performance expectations, developing team members), and her resume shows zero management experience. Her career trajectory is individual-contributor focused (Staff Engineer), not management-track. For a mid-to-senior manager role, the absence of people leadership experience is a critical gap that cannot be overlooked.
strengths: 20 years of full-stack software development across web, mobile, embedded systems, and testing frameworks demonstrates strong technical foundation
Organized code dojos, taught Selenium courses, and built open source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) showing genuine passion for developer productivity and quality
Experience with microservices, CI/CD infrastructure, and distributed systems at scale (handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby) enables hands-on technical guidance
concerns: Zero people management experience; no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or developing team members—a stated minimum requirement
Career progression is individual-contributor track (Staff Engineer); no organizational change leadership or strategic team-building experience demonstrated
Developer tools experience primarily through side projects rather than primary career focus; limited production-level build systems or testing infrastructure specialization

key_factors.factor: Strong technical foundation with developer tools passion
Individual-contributor career orientation vs. management track
